person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Maud of Champaigne

Biography
Matilda or Maud de Campania was born ca. 1325 in Spennithorne, North Yorkshire, England. She was the daughter of Hugo de Campania, b: 1300 in York, Yorkshire, and his wife, Alice (Box) de Campania, b: ca. 1300 in Leicestershire, England.

Sometimes called "Maud of Champaigne" or "Matilda de Campania," she had 2 husbands:

1st m, John FitzRanulf de Lassell, 1325-1348; m: ca. 1343

Children:
1.) Ranulf FitzJohn, ca. 1345 - ca. 1388; eldest son

2.) Barbara (Lassell) Stansfield, ca. 1346 -

2nd m, Robert de Hilton, Kt., 1326 - 1364; m: ca. 1348

Children:
1.) Robert de Hilton II, ca. 1350, at Swine, Yorkshire

2.) John de Hilton, ca. 1352, at Swine, Yorkshire

3.) Maud (de Hilton) Constable, ca. 1356, Swine, Yorks

4.) William de Hilton, ca. 1360, at Swine, Yorkshire

5.) Dionisia de Hilton, ca. 1363, at Swine, Yorkshire

Maud de Campania passed away about 1369 at Swine, East Yorkshire, England. Her 2nd husband was "Robert de Hilton, Kt., Lord of Swine".

sourceList.title: Archbishop William Zouche of York's Registers

zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda Campania於1325年出生在英國英格蘭諾斯 越可爾斯尼恩時，她的父親Hugo de Campania25歲，母親Alice Box24歲。她於1340年與Robert Hilton在英國越可爾斯文結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年8月在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁斯文享年24歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁斯文。

nameMeanings.name: Campania
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Mario, Angelo, Gino, Alfonso, Antonio, Biagio, Caesar, Dino, Falco, Sisto, Alessandra, Amerigo, Carlo, Cosmo.  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jorge, Luis, Roberto, Agustin, Amado.

Italian and Spanish: metonymic occupational name for a bell-ringer or bell-maker, from campana ‘bell’ (abbreviated from the Latin phrase vasa campana ‘Campanian vessels’, since bells were first produced in Campania).

variants.name: Campanella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Santo, Sal, Rocco, Calogero, Cosmo, Domenic, Vito, Aldo, Annamarie, Antonio.

Italian: from a diminutive of campana ‘bell’, applied as a metonymic occupational name for a maker of small bells or handbells, or as a means of distinguishing one branch or member of a family called Campana from another.

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda Campania was born in 1325, in Spennithorne, North Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugo de Campania, was 25 and her mother, Alice Box, was 24. She married Robert Hilton in 1340, in Swine, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in August 1349, in Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 24, and was buried in Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Robert Hilton en 1340, à Swine, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en août 1349, à Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 24 ans, et a été enterrée à Swine, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

Sie hat 1340 in Swine, Yorkshire, England Robert Hilton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. ja.Swine, Yorkshire, England: イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングスウィン
ja.lifeSummary: Matilda Campaniaが1325年にイギリスイギリスノース ヨークシュアイアスペニソルンで生まれた時，父Hugo de Campaniaは25歳，母Alice Boxは24歳であった。1340年にイギリスヨークシュアイアスウィンでRobert Hiltonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1349年8月にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングスウィンで24歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングスウィンにて埋葬された。

ko.Swine, Yorkshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스윈
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda Campania(이)가 1325년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스 요르크실르 스펜이토른에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugo de Campania(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Box(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1340년 영국 요르크실르 스윈에서 Robert Hilton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스윈에서 24세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스윈에 묻혔다.

ru.Swine, Yorkshire, England: сваин, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda Campania родилась в 1325 г. в местности спеннитаурн, норт еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Hugo de Campania было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Box, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Hilton в 1340 г. в местности сваин, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла август 1349 г. в местности сваин, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 24 лет и была похоронена в местности сваин, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
